Mostly jokes but seriously, for anyone who thinks being an Olympian means youre making lots of money. Thats not true for most sports. Many if us take a second job or rely on parents help us chase our dreams. And even when you make it there, especially if youre not a popular sport, the hype fizzles out and youre left to make it happen again.  I LOVE MY JOB, but it takes a lot more than what you see on social media or the end result at the Games. Support your local Olympians!

Just a girl trying to get us the recognition we deserve.  Ive trained for the last 10 years on national team for 8 hours a day 6 days a week with a 15-30 minute break. Many times doing 10 to 12 hour days on our heaviest days  #artisticswimming #swimmingpool

Value in sport should be measured in how much you learn about YOU!!! Your own growth as a person and the lifelong friendships you find are priceless. In the end when you retire, which may have already happened or will happen later, youre bigger than youre sport. But the lessons and values of hard work will carry into everything in your life. Measure yourself on how you keep showing up, even when its hard and impossible or the day before went horribly.  YOU show up for YOU.

I hope this resonates with someone struggling with a set back. This June itll be 2 years since Qualifiers. Ive learned so much since then and though it didnt happen at first, ive redirected my life to being more dedicated to my passion than ive ever been in my life. These past 2 years taught me just how lucky I am to have found my passion, because not everyone does. So ill hold onto my Olympic dream, and see it to the end. Because theres no such thing as failure.
